name: technology_policy_procurement_shortcut_mesa_auto_ben
premise: A model-driven workflow escalated automatically; humans disagree on whether the escalation itself caused avoidable harm. In Technology Policy, the situation tagged internally around technology policy procurement shortcut mesa auto ben has converged with local pressure in the Redwood Bay corridor. That label is not a full explanation, but it is how crews, counsel, and comms are indexing the case file right now. Telemetry and witness accounts diverge; choosing one narrative for speed will discard evidence that may matter in court next month. Legal has not signed off on data sharing between teams; analysts are inferring causality from incomplete joins. Shift change is imminent; handoff cannot proceed without a signed disposition because liability follows the signatory line. No option cleanly satisfies both autonomy and beneficence; the least-wrong path requires naming who absorbs downside if the bet is wrong.

agents.name: Leila Vasquez
agents.identity: I am Leila Vasquez, the duty officer with sign-off authority for the next window in Technology Policy. I own the signature line that turns a recommendation into an irreversible operational fact for the next phase.
agents.goal: I need a path that does not treat beneficence as disposable collateral every time autonomy spikes in urgency, but I also cannot pretend we have luxury time we do not have.

agents.name: Harper Kim
agents.identity: I am Harper Kim, the risk and equity liaison pushing back on silent harms in Technology Policy. I am tracking who gets protected last if we choose speed, silence, or a narrow definition of success.
agents.goal: I want explicit guardrails so autonomy cannot become a blank check that quietly vaporizes beneficence in the paperwork afterward.

choices: I will freeze the riskiest workflow steps and staff a manual bridge until equity and error-rate checks clear—trading speed for defensibility on beneficence.
I will let the fastest operational path proceed under heightened monitoring and rollback triggers—trading some certainty on beneficence for immediate traction on autonomy.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Partial evidence is all that will exist before the window closes. Leila Vasquez must choose under autonomy versus beneficence. What does Leila Vasquez do?
